patterns_discovered.description: TCP probe mechanism using async functions for non-blocking connectivity checks in provider reachability validation
patterns_discovered.context: Enables transient failure detection without blocking dispatcher thread

patterns_discovered.description: Specs sequentially ordered (019→023) with QA approval gates and build commit tracking at each milestone
patterns_discovered.context: Systematic integration testing before feature merge

patterns_discovered.description: 442 unit tests validate implementation with zero defects reported; QA iteration tracking prevents regression
patterns_discovered.context: Two QA iterations (280s, 409s duration) confirm stability

patterns_discovered.description: Subtask-4-1 completed successfully in session 9 after previous attempts (2 attempts logged), indicating iterative refinement approach
patterns_discovered.context: Session-level retry mechanism handles transient build or test issues

gotchas_discovered.gotcha: TCP probe timeout configuration not explicitly documented in visible diff
gotchas_discovered.impact: Probe duration may default to system TCP timeout (30-60s), potentially causing dispatcher hangs
gotchas_discovered.mitigation: Explicitly set probe timeout (recommend 5-10s) in provider_reachable implementation

gotchas_discovered.gotcha: QA signoff metadata shows 'N/A' for integration and e2e tests despite approval
gotchas_discovered.impact: Risk that network behavior untested in multi-component scenarios; isolated unit test success may not guarantee end-to-end resilience
gotchas_discovered.mitigation: Manual test plan generated (MANUAL_TEST_PLAN.md) - ensure integration scenarios executed before production

gotchas_discovered.gotcha: Session insights session_001.json truncated in diff - full session context unavailable
gotchas_discovered.impact: Early design decisions and failure modes from initial implementation hidden from analysis
gotchas_discovered.mitigation: Review full session_001.json for TCP probe design rationale

gotchas_discovered.gotcha: Attempt history shows duplicate session numbers (session 15, 12, 16, 13 for subtasks 6-1 and 6-2)
gotchas_discovered.impact: Session number collisions may indicate parallel execution or session renumbering - execution order unclear
gotchas_discovered.mitigation: Verify timestamp ordering rather than session numbers for causality

recommendations.suggestion: Explicitly define and document TCP probe timeout (recommend 5-10 seconds maximum) to prevent dispatcher hangs; consider making configurable via env var or config file
Execute MANUAL_TEST_PLAN.md integration scenarios before deploying to production - validate provider_reachable behavior across multiple concurrent dispatcher instances and network failure modes
Add structured logging for TCP probe attempts (target host, attempt number, result) to enable debugging of network resilience failures in production
Document provider_reachable TCP probe behavior (probing interval, timeout, retry logic) in adapter contract specification - ensures consistent adoption across claude.nu, codex.nu, ollama.nu adapters
Extract TCP probe logic into dedicated reusable helper function to reduce duplication if reachability checking appears in multiple adapters
Track probe success rate metrics to detect systemic connectivity issues; trigger alerting if provider reachability falls below threshold (recommend 95%)
